title: Post by theneurofactory
description:
A $2 veterinary pill is doing what $10,000/month cancer drugs can't.

It's called fenbendazole.

It was designed to deworm animals. But researchers discovered it kills cancer cells through multiple mechanisms — starving them of glucose, stopping them from dividing, and forcing them to self-destruct.

In 2016, Joe Tippens was given 3 months to live with terminal lung cancer. He started taking fenbendazole alongside vitamin E, CBD oil, and curcumin. Three months later, his PET scan came back clean. Zero cancer cells detected.

He was the only one cured out of 1,100 patients in his clinical trial.

Since then, lab studies have shown fenbendazole works against lung, breast, colorectal, prostate, cervical, and pancreatic cancer cells.

A 2025 case series documented three Stage IV cancer patients achieving complete or near-complete remission after self-administering fenbendazole — without chemotherapy.

So why haven't you heard about this?

Because the patent expired in the 1990s. No pharma company will spend billions running trials on a drug they can't own. There's no ROI. So the research stays small, underfunded, and ignored.

Meanwhile, cancer drugs that cost families their life savings get fast-tracked through approval.

This post isn't medical advice. It's information that should be public. The science exists. The case reports exist. The mechanisms are well understood.

What's missing is funding. Ask yourself why.

Save this. Send it to someone who needs it.
